name: Lalita Tripurasundari
shortDescription: Lalita Tripurasundari is a principal Hindu goddess, revered as the supreme manifestation of Adi Shakti, the divine feminine energy. She embodies beauty, bliss, and the ultimate reality.
description: Lalita Tripurasundari, also known as Shodashi, is a central deity in the Srikula tradition of Shaktism, representing the supreme goddess and the ultimate form of Shakti. Her name, 'Lalita,' signifies 'she who plays' or 'the beautiful one,' reflecting her joyful and playful nature. 'Tripurasundari' means 'the beauty of the three worlds,' highlighting her sovereignty over the physical, astral, and causal realms. She is often depicted as a sixteen-year-old girl, symbolizing perfection and eternal youth. Lalita Tripurasundari is associated with the Sri Chakra, a complex geometric diagram used in tantric worship, which represents the cosmos and the human body. Her worship is believed to grant spiritual liberation, material prosperity, and fulfillment of desires. The Lalita Sahasranama, a hymn containing one thousand names of the goddess, is a key text in her veneration, describing her attributes, powers, and cosmic significance. She is considered the source of all other goddesses and is sometimes identified with Parvati, the consort of Shiva. In some traditions, she is believed to have emerged from the sacrificial fire (yajna kunda) performed by the gods to destroy the demon Bhandasura. Her mantras and yantras are considered highly potent for spiritual practice.
